data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260514T092041-0bfe506b/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260514T092041-0bfe506b/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260514T092041-0bfe506b/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260514T092041-0bfe506b/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260514T092041-0bfe506b/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-c2e7f14b">
    <title>integrate fizeau v0.13.1 (routing hard-gate fix)</title>
    <description>
fizeau v0.13.1 is released and contains the autorouting fix the ddx workers were stalling on. Bump the ddx-side fizeau pin to v0.13.1 and restart the ddx work / ddx server processes so they pick up the new binary.

Context: with v0.13.0, every first dispatch attempt after a host (e.g. bragi) went off-network burned the full ~165s retry budget (5 attempts × ~30s dial timeout + exponential backoff) before the routing layer learned the host was unreachable. v0.13.0's hard-gate (`Inputs.ProviderUnreachable`) only populated from snapshot discovery errors, which lagged the first dispatch failure.

v0.13.1 closes two gaps:
- Route-attempt failures with dial-class errors (dial tcp / connection refused / no route to host / i/o timeout / no such host / 502 Bad Gateway / 503 / 504) now populate `Inputs.ProviderUnreachable` immediately. One failed dispatch hard-gates the provider for the cooldown TTL on the very next call. Non-dial failures (context canceled, 4xx, wall-clock timeouts) stay as soft demotion so sticky-lease keeps working through single transient hiccups.
- New `RefreshIfStale` snapshot refresh mode + `Cache.MaybeRefreshSync` primitive. `buildRoutingInputs` now uses it so a cold-start route call re-probes stale providers in-line (bounded by 5s discovery timeout per source) before scoring. The first call after a host dies is now at worst the 5s probe rather than the full retry budget.

Release: https://github.com/easel/fizeau/releases/tag/v0.13.1
Commit: 52c436e8 (fix routing) + the v0.13.1 tag.

Integration steps:
1. Update whatever pins the ddx workers carry on the fizeau SDK / agent / binary to v0.13.1 (go.mod replace block, install.sh hash, agent_binary_url, whatever the actual mechanism is).
2. Rebuild / reinstall ddx workers.
3. Restart any long-running `ddx work --watch`, `ddx server`, or similar processes so they load the new binary.
4. Verify by killing bragi (or pointing at an off host) and watching `ddx work` — instead of ~165s of `WARN provider error, retrying attempt=N` against bragi, the router should pick an alternative on the second-or-later request without burning the retry budget.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. ddx production code references fizeau at version v0.13.1 (or higher within the v0.13.x series). Verified by `grep -rEn "github.com/easel/fizeau (v0\.13\.[1-9]|v0\.1[4-9])" ~/Projects/ddx/cli ~/Projects/ddx/go.* 2>/dev/null | wc -l` returning at least 1.
2. The installed `ddx` binary on PATH carries the v0.13.1 routing symbol `ProviderUnreachable`. Verified by `strings $(which ddx) 2>/dev/null | grep -c "ProviderUnreachable"` returning a value >= 1.
3. The v0.13.0 cooldown→hard-gate bug is no longer reproducible: after restarting `ddx work --watch` against a deliberately-off provider, no more than one `WARN provider error, retrying attempt=4` line per provider appears in `~/Projects/ddx/.ddx/logs/ddx-server.log` over a 5-minute observation window. Verified by `bash -c 'awk -F"\"" "/WARN provider error, retrying attempt=4/{n[\$4]++} END{for(p in n) if (n[p] > 1) exit 1; exit 0}" ~/Projects/ddx/.ddx/logs/ddx-server.log' && echo OK` printing OK after a 5-minute soak.
4. ddx test suite is green at integration HEAD: `cd ~/Projects/ddx && go test ./cli/... -count=1 -timeout 5m` exits 0.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:routing, kind:integration, dep:fizeau</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="345308a031983ccc3c20f2ba8966f4ba15596d56" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260514T092041-0bfe506b"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Wrote `no_changes_rationale.txt` with `status: proposed`. Summary:

- **Code change is already merged.** Commit `325c45b35` ("chore(agent): update fizeau to v0.13.1") bumped `cli/go.mod` to v0.13.1. AC#1's grep returns 3 matches against HEAD.
- **AC#2 / AC#3 require operator action outside the worktree** — installing the binary onto PATH and restarting long-running `ddx work --watch` / `ddx server` daemons, then a 5-minute log soak against a deliberately-off provider.
- **AC#4 has unrelated pre-existing failures.** After `make frontend-build`, `internal/server -short` fails on `TestServerWorker_WiresPreClaimIntakeHook` (expects "lint", gets "triage" — workers.go hook-ordering regression unrelated to fizeau). Non-short mode hangs on `TestMCPAgentModels` real-network probes.

No source edits were made — all routing changes live inside fizeau v0.13.1, which the go.mod pin already pulls in.
